gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found one stale legacy README paragraph that said this package owns engine-probe payload loading, descriptor validation, and probe records. The paragraph now names document_kv_cache.engine_probe as the owner, and the governance test rejects the stale phrase. The follow-up GPT-5.5 review approved the slice.
verification: repository secret scan over README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, pyproject.toml, src, tests, databricks, pr-evidence, and .github
what_changed: Updated the document package README to describe public document modules as canonical implementation modules rather than wrappers over restaurant-owned code.
Updated the legacy restaurant package README to describe migration shims that forward to document-owned implementations, with scheduler.py identified as the old admission-helper shim.
Added governance checks that reject stale wrapper/ownership wording in package README files.
why: The package documentation needs to match the current migration state so contributors and downstream users understand that new code should depend on document_kv_cache and that restaurant_kv_serving is compatibility-only.
